critic_raw: The English-only evaluation is insufficient for a vendor whose product serves multilingual customers. The model architecture can process multiple languages, so an English-only evaluation represents a choice to test in the easiest context. The fairness appendix is also incomplete. Together these gaps make the benchmark look like it was designed to produce favorable results rather than to comprehensively characterize model behavior.
defender_raw: The product launch is English-only. Non-English messages are excluded from the model by routing policy. The evaluation matches the deployment: future-month English test set, English annotations, English success criteria. The fairness appendix covers what is observable within the deployment population. A multilingual annotation study is the pre-specified prerequisite for any future expansion. The English-only evaluation is not the easiest context — it is the correct context for the English-only launch.

debate_rounds.critic: The key question is whether the English-only deployment restriction is genuine and documented before the evaluation was designed. If the product operations team genuinely specified English-only launch before the evaluation was structured, then the English-only evaluation is the correct scope. I concede that if the deployment restriction is documented, this is not cherry-picking but appropriate scope matching.
debate_rounds.defender: The prompt states the team pre-specified that deployment would remain English-only until a separate multilingual effort was completed — before training. The evaluation was then designed for that English-only scope. This is the correct order: deployment decision first, evaluation design second. The documentation should show this sequence.

adjudication_raw: The critic concedes after one round when the pre-specification sequence is clarified. Defense wins.
all_issues_raised: English-only evaluation appearing to cherry-pick favorable scope — resolved by pre-specified deployment restriction

empirical_test.condition: Verify pre-specification of English-only deployment precedes training and evaluation design; confirm future-month English test set represents the deployment population; confirm report does not generalize to non-English contexts
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Deployment restriction decided after reviewing multilingual performance data, or model applied to non-English messages
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Pre-specification documented before training; English-only restriction consistently maintained; future-month test set represents English population
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Deployment restriction appears prospectively chosen but documentation sequence is incomplete
